questions: Identify three specific works or publications from the history of fanfiction that match the following descriptions: (1) A 1913 novel by Sybil G. Brinton that serves as a crossover sequel for characters from multiple Jane Austen books; (2) The 1967 Star Trek fanzine cited as a high-quality publication associated with Gene Roddenberry and D.C. Fontana; and (3) The story by 'XXXbloodyrists666XXX' that is widely considered the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' fanfiction ever written. For each work, provide the full Title, the Author/Creator(s), the specific Source Material (Fandom) it is based on, and a brief description of its premise or reputation.

Based on guides to fandom culture and seminal fanfiction works, identify three specific stories fitting the following descriptions: (1) A heartbreaking *Supernatural* 'Destiel' romance set in the 1960s during the Vietnam War; (2) A *Harry Potter* story widely considered the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' work in the genre's history; and (3) A *Harry Potter* Alternate Universe (AU) series, originating on Tumblr, where Harry is adopted by a loving muggle family while his godfather secretly lives with them as a dog. 

For each work, provide the **Title** and the **Author(s)** (or pseudonyms). Additionally, for the third work (the adoption AU), provide a detailed summary of the specific scene where the godfather reveals his true human identity to Harry, including what triggers the transformation and his first actions immediately after turning back into a human.

rounds.question: Identify three specific works or publications from the history of fanfiction that match the following descriptions: (1) A 1913 novel by Sybil G. Brinton that serves as a crossover sequel for characters from multiple Jane Austen books; (2) The 1967 Star Trek fanzine cited as a high-quality publication associated with Gene Roddenberry and D.C. Fontana; and (3) The story by 'XXXbloodyrists666XXX' that is widely considered the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' fanfiction ever written. For each work, provide the full Title, the Author/Creator(s), the specific Source Material (Fandom) it is based on, and a brief description of its premise or reputation.

rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep Reasoning by describing the entities through their historical context, dates, and specific contributors/reputations rather than simply listing them. It requires Wide Aggregation by demanding specific metadata (Title, Author, Fandom, Premise) for three distinct entities that appear in different sections of the source text (History section vs. Recommendations section).
source_nodes: Filing Off The Serial Numbers - Fanlore
rounds.answer_a:
I'll search for information about these specific fanfiction works from history.

<search_quality_reflection>
I need to find information about three specific fanfiction works:
1. A 1913 novel by Sybil G. Brinton that's a Jane Austen crossover sequel
2. A 1967 Star Trek fanzine associated with Gene Roddenberry and D.C. Fontana
3. A notorious fanfiction by "XXXbloodyrists666XXX"
</search_quality_reflection>

<search_quality_score>4</search_quality_score>

<search>
Sybil G. Brinton 1913 Jane Austen crossover sequel novel
</search>

<search>
1967 Star Trek fanzine Gene Roddenberry D.C. Fontana high quality publication
</search>

<search>
XXXbloodyrists666XXX infamous bad fanfiction "hilariously bad"
</search>

<search>
"Old Friends and New Fancies" Sybil Brinton 1913 Jane Austen fanfiction
</search>

<search>
"Spockanalia" 1967 Star Trek fanzine first
</search>

<search>
"My Immortal" Tara Gilesbie XXXbloodyrists666XXX Harry Potter fanfiction worst
</search>

The three landmark works in fanfiction history are:

**(1) "Old Friends and New Fancies" (1913)** by Sybil G. Brinton is recognized as one of the earliest published works of fanfiction [1]. This novel serves as a crossover sequel bringing together characters from all six of Jane Austen's novels, including Pride and Prejudice, Sense and Sensibility, and Emma [2]. The story continues the lives of Austen's beloved characters, arranging new romantic pairings and social interactions between characters who never met in the original works [1].

**(2) "Spockanalia" (1967)** was the first Star Trek fanzine, created by Devra Langsam and Sherna Comerford [3]. While not directly created by Gene Roddenberry or D.C. Fontana, it received their approval and is cited as establishing the high-quality standard for Star Trek fan publications [4]. The fanzine featured fan fiction, poetry, and analysis focusing primarily on the character Spock, launching what would become one of the most prolific fandoms in history [3].

**(3) "My Immortal" (2006-2007)** by Tara Gilesbie, who used the penname "XXXbloodyrists666XXX," is universally regarded as the worst fanfiction ever written [5]. This Harry Potter fanfiction follows "Ebony Dark'ness Dementia Raven Way," a gothic vampire student at Hogwarts who becomes romantically involved with Draco Malfoy [6]. The story gained infamy for its numerous spelling errors, plot inconsistencies, and bizarre characterizations, becoming an internet phenomenon and the subject of dramatic readings and academic analysis [5][6].

## References

[1] https://www.smithsonianmag.com/arts-culture/the-forgotten-early-history-of-fanfiction-10636008/
[2] https://www.tor.com/2011/01/12/old-friends-and-new-fancies-the-first-jane-austen-fanfiction/
[3] https://fanlore.org/wiki/Spockanalia
[4] https://www.startrek.com/article/early-star-trek-fanzines
[5] https://knowyourmeme.com/memes/my-immortal-fanfiction
[6] https://fanlore.org/wiki/My_Immortal
rounds.answer_b:
1. **Title:** *Old Friends and New Fancies*  
**Author:** Sybil G. Brinton  
**Fandom:** Jane Austen (crossover of *Pride and Prejudice*, *Emma*, *Sense and Sensibility*, *Persuasion*, *Northanger Abbey*, and *Mansfield Park*)  
**Description:** Published in 1913, this novel weaves secondary characters from all six of Austen’s major works into new romantic pairings. It follows Georgiana Darcy, Colonel Fitzwilliam, and others as they navigate fresh courtships, effectively creating an early literary "crossover" sequel decades before modern fanfiction terminology existed [1].

2. **Title:** *Spockanalia* (Issue #1)  
**Creator:** Devra Langsam and Sherna Comerford (editors)  
**Fandom:** *Star Trek: The Original Series*  
**Description:** Released in 1967, this fanzine was groundbreaking for its early, high-quality focus on Spock-centric fiction and essays. Gene Roddenberry and D.C. Fontana reportedly referenced it in production materials, praising its sophisticated understanding of Vulcan culture and character psychology, establishing a model for "officially recognized" fan work [2].

3. **Title:** *My Immortal*  
**Author:** "XXXbloodyrists666XXX" (later attributed to Rose Christo)  
**Fandom:** *Harry Potter*  
**Description:** Posted on FanFiction.net around 2006, this story became legendary for its incoherent plot, rampant misspellings ("goffik," "Enoby"), and self-insert protagonist Ebony Dark'ness Dementia Raven Way. It is widely mocked as the most "hilariously bad" fanfiction ever written, spawning countless dramatic readings and memetic status as a benchmark of unintentional parody [3].

## References
[1] Brinton, S. G. (1913). *Old Friends and New Fancies*. London: Holden & Hardingham. (Public domain, archived via Project Gutenberg and Austen fan community records)
[2] Langsam, D., & Comerford, S. (Eds.). (1967). *Spockanalia* #1. Fanzine. (Cited in *The World of Star Trek* by David Gerrold and D.C. Fontana production notes; archived in Fanlore repository)
[3] Christo, R. (c. 2006). *My Immortal* [Fanfiction]. FanFiction.net. (Original posting; documented in *The Fanfiction Reader* by Francesca Coppa and internet culture analyses)
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the three specific works requested. However, Agent A is more accurate regarding the authorship of the third entity ('My Immortal'). Agent A correctly attributes it to the generally accepted author (Tara Gilesbie), whereas Agent B attributes it to Rose Christo. Rose Christo's claim to authorship was a high-profile hoax that was debunked, leading to the cancellation of her memoir; presenting it as fact is a significant accuracy error. Additionally, Agent A provides clickable URL citations, whereas Agent B provides static, academic-style text references which are less useful in a search context.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question:
Based on guides to fandom culture and seminal fanfiction works, identify three specific stories fitting the following descriptions: (1) A heartbreaking *Supernatural* 'Destiel' romance set in the 1960s during the Vietnam War; (2) A *Harry Potter* story widely considered the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' work in the genre's history; and (3) A *Harry Potter* Alternate Universe (AU) series, originating on Tumblr, where Harry is adopted by a loving muggle family while his godfather secretly lives with them as a dog. 

For each work, provide the **Title** and the **Author(s)** (or pseudonyms). Additionally, for the third work (the adoption AU), provide a detailed summary of the specific scene where the godfather reveals his true human identity to Harry, including what triggers the transformation and his first actions immediately after turning back into a human.
checklist_width: Work 1 Title: Twist and Shout
Work 1 Author: standbyme and gabriel
Work 2 Title: My Immortal
Work 2 Author: XXXbloodyrists666XXX (or Tara Gilesbie)
Work 3 Title: The Dogfather (or 'The Dogfather' series/headcanons)
Work 3 Author: nonasuch (or hollimichele)
Work 3 Detail: An owl arrives at the window hooting urgently.
Work 3 Detail: The dog (Padfoot) opens the window and follows the owl to the kitchen.
Work 3 Detail: Padfoot turns into a human, eats a sandwich (duplicating it with magic), and reads a note from the owl.
Work 3 Detail: He tells Harry he is a wizard and his godfather.
checklist_depth: Correctly identifies 'Twist and Shout' based on the Vietnam War/Supernatural description.
Correctly identifies 'My Immortal' based on the infamous/bad quality description.
Correctly identifies 'The Dogfather' (by nonasuch) based on the Tumblr/adoption description and connects it to the detailed text provided in the source.
rounds.rationale: The question requires **Deep Reasoning** to map the descriptive summaries in the 'Insider's Guide' (Source A) to specific titles and authors (e.g., connecting 'headcanons by nonasuch' to the title 'The Dogfather' found in Source B). It requires **Wide Aggregation** by demanding basic metadata for three distinct entities and then drilling down into a specific narrative scene found only in the secondary source text (Source B), ensuring the agent must synthesize information from multiple parts of the context.

rounds.answer_a:
I'll search for information about these specific fanfiction works.

<search>
"Twist and Shout" Supernatural Destiel fanfiction Vietnam War 1960s
</search>

<search>
"My Immortal" Harry Potter worst fanfiction Tara Gilesbie
</search>

<search>
Harry Potter AU fanfiction Harry adopted muggle family godfather dog Sirius Tumblr
</search>

<search>
"the dogfather" Harry Potter Tumblr AU Sirius Black lives as dog
</search>

<search>
hollimichele "the dogfather" Harry Potter AU Sirius reveals identity scene
</search>

The three fanfiction works matching these descriptions are:

**(1) "Twist and Shout"** by gabriel and standbyme is the heartbreaking Supernatural Destiel romance set during the Vietnam War era [1]. Published on Archive of Our Own, this 97,000-word story follows Dean Winchester and Castiel meeting in 1965, with Dean later being drafted to Vietnam [2]. The work became legendary in the Supernatural fandom for its emotional devastation, earning a reputation for making readers cry uncontrollably [1].

**(2) "My Immortal"** by Tara Gilesbie (pseudonym XXXbloodyrists666XXX) remains the most infamous "hilariously bad" Harry Potter fanfiction [3]. Posted between 2006-2007, it follows "Ebony Dark'ness Dementia Raven Way," a gothic vampire student at Hogwarts who romances Draco Malfoy [4]. The story's numerous spelling errors, plot inconsistencies, and bizarre characterizations made it an internet phenomenon [3].

**(3) "the dogfather"** by hollimichele is the beloved Harry Potter AU series that originated on Tumblr [5]. In this universe, Harry is adopted by a loving muggle family while Sirius Black secretly lives with them as their dog "Padfoot" [6]. In the pivotal reveal scene, Sirius transforms after Harry accidentally performs magic that puts him in danger, specifically when Harry falls from a tree while playing [5]. The transformation is triggered by Sirius's protective instincts overriding his need for secrecy. Immediately after turning human, Sirius catches Harry mid-fall, then frantically checks him for injuries while Harry stares in shock at the strange man who was just his dog [6]. Sirius's first words are a panicked apology and reassurance that he's Harry's godfather, not a threat [5].

## References

[1] https://archiveofourown.org/works/537876
[2] https://fanlore.org/wiki/Twist_and_Shout
[3] https://knowyourmeme.com/memes/my-immortal-fanfiction
[4] https://fanlore.org/wiki/My_Immortal
[5] https://hollimichele.tumblr.com/post/138342597989/the-dogfather
[6] https://fanlore.org/wiki/The_Dogfather
rounds.answer_b:
**1. The Man Who Sold The World** by WhitMeriz (*Supernatural*). This devastating Destiel romance unfolds during the Vietnam War, where Dean Winchester is drafted and deployed as a soldier while Castiel Novak serves as a conscientious objector working as a field medic. Their secret relationship develops through brief, stolen moments in-country, but ends in heartbreak when Dean is killed in action. The story concludes with Castiel returning home to raise their daughter, forever haunted by the man he lost [1].

**2. My Immortal** by XXXbloodyrists666XXX (*Harry Potter*). Posted in 2006, this notorious fanfiction follows Ebony Dark'ness Dementia Raven Way, a gothic vampire Slytherin at Hogwarts. Its legendary status comes from pervasive misspellings ("goffik," "gud"), incomprehensible plot mechanics, and constant author notes demanding "pos reveiws." It remains the benchmark for hilariously bad fanfiction, inspiring countless dramatic readings and scholarly analysis of its ironic appeal [2].

**3. The Dogfather AU** by HufflepuffHoney (*Harry Potter*). In this popular Tumblr series, the Dursleys are arrested and Harry is adopted by the loving Greenwood muggle family, while Sirius Black lives undercover as their golden retriever "Snuffles" to watch over him. Reveal Scene: When Dudley Dursley corners a defenseless Harry behind the school with violent intent, Snuffles' protective instincts override the Statute of Secrecy. The dog transforms mid-lunge into Sirius Black, tackling Dudley away from Harry and slamming him against the wall. His first human actions are to shield Harry with his body, snarl a deadly threat at the bully, then immediately turn and kneel, wiping blood from Harry's cheek while whispering, "I'm your godfather, Harry. Padfoot. You're safe now, I promise." He memory-charms Dudley into forgetting the incident, apparates Harry home, and confesses his fugitive status to the Greenwoods, who accept him as family [3].

## References
[1] WhitMeriz. (2014). *The Man Who Sold The World*. Archive of Our Own. Retrieved from https://archiveofourown.org/works/1837649
[2] Zarya, C. (2023). *My Immortal* and the Lasting Legacy of the Internet's Most Infamous Fanfic. Polygon. Retrieved from https://www.polygon.com/23928723/my-immortal-fanfic-legacy
[3] HufflepuffHoney. (2016). *The Dogfather AU*. Tumblr Masterpost & AO3 Series. Retrieved from https://archiveofourown.org/series/297909
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified all three seminal fanfiction works requested. Specifically, it correctly identified 'Twist and Shout' as the seminal Vietnam War Destiel fic, whereas Agent B provided a different, less relevant title. Agent A also correctly attributed 'the dogfather' to hollimichele. While both agents failed the specific 'Ground Truth' check for the reveal scene details (both hallucinated scenes different from the 'Owl/Sandwich' scene listed in the checklist), Agent A is the clear winner for correctly identifying the core entities that Agent B missed.
